result:
Stage 1 complete. Extracted SvelteKit (@sveltejs/kit 2.57.1) routing concepts:

- **Route definition**: file-based; path + hash history
- **Syntax**: static, dynamic `[slug]`, optional `[[lang]]`, rest `[...file]`, matchers `[p=fruit]`, groups `(app)`, nested layouts
- **SSR**: supported; detect via `browser` from `$app/environment`
- **Hooks**: `beforeNavigate`, `onNavigate` (post-load, pre-DOM), `afterNavigate`, plus reactive `page` / `navigating` from `$app/state` (rune-based since 2.12) and legacy `$app/stores`
- **Versions**: 8 routing-relevant entries (2.0 → 2.57.1) including $app/state (2.12), hash routing (2.14), async reroute (2.18), scroll target (2.51)

examples.note: Static path. File at src/routes/about/+page.svelte. page.route.id equals '/about'.
Dynamic segment. params.slug === 'hello-world'. page.route.id preserves the bracket pattern.
Optional segment. Also matches /en/home where params.lang === 'en'. When absent, params.lang is undefined. Cannot follow a rest parameter.
Rest/catch-all. params.file === 'docs/routing.md'. Matches empty when route is /a/[...rest]/z accessed as /a/z.
Matcher. src/params/fruit.js exports match(param) => boolean. Route id retains the '=fruit' qualifier.
Route group. Parentheses DO NOT appear in the URL but DO appear in page.route.id. Used to apply a shared (app)/+layout.svelte without affecting the URL.
Nested route. The layout at /settings wraps the child page. page.route.id is the leaf route id.
Hex-escaped characters in route folder names for filesystem-incompatible characters like ':' and ')'.

clientDetection.value: import { browser } from '$app/environment' — boolean, true when code runs in the browser

hooks.name: beforeNavigate
hooks.access: Import { beforeNavigate } from '$app/navigation'. MUST be called during Svelte component initialization; stays active while the component is mounted.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — current route's { params, route: { id }, url, scroll } before navigation.

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — target route's { params, route: { id }, url, scroll }. May be null for external navigations (willUnload=true).

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: 'enter' | 'form' | 'leave' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate' — trigger category. beforeNavigate may observe 'leave' for full-page unloads.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: boolean — true if navigation will cause a full-page unload (e.g. external link, reload).

availableApi.name: navigation.delta
availableApi.description: number — only present when type === 'popstate'; history delta for back/forward.

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — resolves when navigation finishes (or rejects if it fails).

availableApi.name: navigation.cancel()
availableApi.description: Function that aborts the navigation. If called, onNavigate/afterNavigate never fire for this attempt.

hooks.name: onNavigate
hooks.access: Import { onNavigate } from '$app/navigation'. MUST be called during component initialization. Callback may return a Promise (awaited before DOM swap) or a cleanup function (called after DOM swap). Intended for View Transitions.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget — previous route's { params, route: { id }, url, scroll }.

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget — final resolved route after any redirects. willUnload is guaranteed false at this point.

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: 'form' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate' — excludes 'enter' and 'leave' (no full-page navigations).

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — resolves when navigation finishes.

hooks.name: afterNavigate
hooks.access: Import { afterNavigate } from '$app/navigation'. MUST be called during component initialization. Fires on initial mount AND after every subsequent client-side navigation.

availableApi.name: navigation.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — null on initial page load (navigation.type === 'enter').

availableApi.name: navigation.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget — final resolved route's { params, route: { id }, url, scroll }.

availableApi.name: navigation.type
availableApi.description: 'enter' | 'form' | 'link' | 'goto' | 'popstate' — excludes 'leave'. 'enter' indicates initial page load / hydration.

availableApi.name: navigation.willUnload
availableApi.description: boolean — always false here.

availableApi.name: navigation.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — already resolved by the time this fires.

hooks.name: page (reactive state via $effect)
hooks.access: Import { page } from '$app/state' (Svelte 5+, SvelteKit >= 2.12). Read inside a Svelte $effect or $derived to reactively observe route changes. Must run during component render; on the server, access is limited to rendering (not load functions).

availableApi.name: page.url
availableApi.description: URL — current page URL (pathname, search, hash).

availableApi.name: page.params
availableApi.description: Record<string, string> — resolved route parameters (including rest/optional).

availableApi.name: page.route.id
availableApi.description: string | null — SvelteKit route id, e.g. '/blog/[slug]' or '/(app)/dashboard'. Null when no route matched.

availableApi.name: page.data
availableApi.description: Record<string, any> — merged data from all matched layout/page load functions.

availableApi.name: page.state
availableApi.description: App.PageState — state set via pushState/replaceState (shallow routing).

availableApi.name: page.status
availableApi.description: number — HTTP status of the page (200, 404, 500, ...).

availableApi.name: page.error
availableApi.description: App.Error | null — error object when a load function threw.

hooks.name: navigating (reactive state via $effect)
hooks.access: Import { navigating } from '$app/state' (Svelte 5+). Read inside $effect/$derived. While a navigation is in-flight, navigating is a Navigation object; when idle, its fields are null. Reflects intermediate redirects before the new page commits.

availableApi.name: navigating.from
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — source route target.

availableApi.name: navigating.to
availableApi.description: NavigationTarget | null — target route target (may change during redirects).

availableApi.name: navigating.type
availableApi.description: NavigationType | null — navigation trigger.

availableApi.name: navigating.delta
availableApi.description: number | undefined — present on popstate navigations.

availableApi.name: navigating.complete
availableApi.description: Promise<void> — resolves when the in-flight navigation completes.

hooks.name: page (legacy store via $app/stores)
hooks.access: Import { page } from '$app/stores' — a Svelte readable store. Subscribe via auto-subscription ($page) in a component, or page.subscribe(...) imperatively. On the server, subscription is limited to component initialization. DEPRECATED since SvelteKit 2.12 in favor of $app/state.

availableApi.name: $page.url
availableApi.description: URL — current page URL.

availableApi.name: $page.params
availableApi.description: Record<string, string> — resolved route params.

availableApi.name: $page.route.id
availableApi.description: string | null — matched route id.

availableApi.name: $page.data
availableApi.description: Record<string, any> — merged load data.

availableApi.name: $page.status
availableApi.description: number — HTTP status.

availableApi.name: $page.error
availableApi.description: App.Error | null — page-level error.

routingChanges: error() and redirect() changed from throwable exceptions to commands that must be thrown by the caller.
Top-level promise awaiting removed from load functions.
depends() now requires a ':' prefix in dependency strings.
Dropped support for Svelte 3; minimum Node 18.13+.
Introduced $app/state module (page, navigating, updated) as rune-based replacement for $app/stores (Svelte 5+). $app/stores deprecated.
Added hash-based routing option via config kit.router.type: 'hash' for SPA deployments without URL rewriting.
reroute hook may now be async and receives a fetch function.
Introduced normalizeUrl helper for URL standardization inside reroute.
Navigation objects passed to beforeNavigate/onNavigate/afterNavigate now expose the originating DOM event (PointerEvent for 'link', SubmitEvent for 'form', PopStateEvent for 'popstate').
NavigationTarget now exposes scroll: { x, y } on from/to so callbacks can restore/animate based on scroll position across back/forward.
Reworked client-driven refresh path; breaking changes to query/refresh behavior. TypeScript 6.0 supported.
Current latest release at time of extraction.

structured_output.notes:
SvelteKit is a full metaframework built on Svelte. Routing is file-based and runtime-reflected: every matched route has a stable string id on page.route.id that mirrors the filesystem pattern including bracket syntax ('[slug]', '[[lang]]', '[...rest]', '[p=matcher]') and route groups like '(app)'. The route id is the natural view name for RUM-style integrations because it collapses distinct URLs that share a route (e.g. /blog/foo and /blog/bar both have route.id === '/blog/[slug]').

Lifecycle model: client navigation proceeds beforeNavigate → (optionally cancelled) → load functions run → onNavigate (View Transitions timing, after load, before DOM swap) → DOM updates / component mount → afterNavigate. The reactive primitives page and navigating in '$app/state' (Svelte 5+, rune-based) are the modern way to observe route state; $app/stores is the pre-2.12 store-based equivalent and is deprecated. Both expose the same shape.

SvelteKit supports SSR by default. Client-only code must be gated with `import { browser } from '$app/environment'` or inside onMount/afterNavigate callbacks (which only run in the browser). The hash-router mode (kit.router.type === 'hash') was added in 2.14 — when enabled, route matching is done against the URL fragment rather than the pathname.

The `reroute` hook (src/hooks.js) runs on both server and client before route matching and can rewrite the pathname; integrations that want the 'final' view name should prefer page.route.id (post-reroute, post-redirect) over the raw URL.

Navigation cancellation: beforeNavigate exposes cancel(). Any load function throwing redirect() cancels the in-flight navigation and starts a new one — onNavigate/afterNavigate only observe the surviving, final navigation.
